name: Autonomous Risk Detection and Intervention
problem_statement: Founders often miss subtle execution risks like repeated de-prioritization or silent scope expansion until projects are already significantly delayed.
hypothesis: We believe that by automatically synthesizing Linear execution signals into actionable interventions, we can prevent scope creep before it impacts sprint velocity.

name: Continuous Agentic Self-Improvement and Evaluation
problem_statement: Static AI prompts fail to adapt to specific workspace contexts, leading to generic or inaccurate product management advice that loses user trust.
hypothesis: We believe that using an offline replay and eval synthesis loop to tune agent heuristics will increase the relevance of AI-generated product insights over time.

name: Unified Multi-Channel Decision Capture
problem_statement: Critical product context is lost across disparate communication channels, causing teams to build features based on outdated or misunderstood requirements.
hypothesis: We believe that ingesting decisions from Slack, voice, and email into a centralized replay UI will reduce engineering rework caused by fragmented context.

name: Leverage-Based Opportunity Cost Auditing
problem_statement: Founders struggle with decision fatigue and placebo productivity because they cannot easily distinguish between high-leverage work and low-value overhead tasks.
hypothesis: We believe that classifying founder workflows into Leverage, Neutral, and Overhead categories will increase time spent on high-impact strategic work.

name: Self-Improving Agentic Evaluation Framework
problem_statement: Current agentic behaviors are static and lack a systematic way to learn from past execution failures or track performance trends.
hypothesis: We believe that implementing automated heuristic tuning and semantic search on failure corpuses will reduce agentic regressions and improve intervention relevance.

name: Opportunity-Cost OS for Founders
problem_statement: Founders suffer from decision fatigue and prioritize low-impact tasks due to a lack of visibility into time-based opportunity costs.
hypothesis: We believe that classifying tasks into Leverage, Neutral, and Overhead categories will enable founders to eliminate placebo productivity.

name: Autonomous Execution and Decision Replay Loop
problem_statement: A gap exists between risk identification and execution, leading to stalled progress and low trust in autonomous actions.
hypothesis: We believe that integrating the Jules execution layer with a decision replay UI will allow founders to delegate tasks while maintaining oversight.

name: Unified Multi-Channel Context Ingestion
problem_statement: Critical product signals are scattered across communication channels, resulting in incomplete risk detection by the agentic system.
hypothesis: We believe that expanding context capture to Slack and Notion will provide the agentic system with the signals needed to detect fragmented risks.

name: Autonomous Agent Reliability and Optimization
problem_statement: Static agent prompts and ephemeral evaluation data prevent the system from learning from past performance and safely evolving its decision-making logic.
hypothesis: We believe that implementing automated evaluation synthesis and canary rollouts for heuristics will reduce regression risks and improve agentic decision accuracy over time.

name: Unified Context Ingestion for Risk Detection
problem_statement: Project risks often emerge in unstructured communication channels before they are reflected in formal task tracking systems, leading to delayed interventions.
hypothesis: We believe that expanding data ingestion beyond Linear to include Slack, Notion, and voice capture will allow the Risk Radar to identify project blockers that are currently invisible to the system.

name: Opportunity-Cost Focused Decision Governance
problem_statement: Founders suffer from placebo productivity and decision fatigue because they lack a clear view of the opportunity costs associated with their current task execution.
hypothesis: We believe that classifying tasks into Leverage/Neutral/Overhead (LNO) and requiring human-in-the-loop approval for Jules actions will help founders focus on high-impact work while maintaining control.

name: Real-Time Agent Observability and Interaction
problem_statement: The black box nature of multi-stage agentic pipelines makes it difficult for users to understand why specific interventions were proposed or how the system arrived at a conclusion.
hypothesis: We believe that providing real-time pipeline streaming and a decision replay UI will increase founder trust in the autonomous execution layer.

name: Real-Time Agent Observability and Interaction
target_segment: Product Founders and Engineers using autonomous agents
problem_statement: Autonomous agents often operate as "black boxes," making it difficult to debug drift or intervene in real-time, leading to wasted spend and misaligned execution.

name: Continuous Agentic Self-Improvement and Evaluation
target_segment: AI Platform and Product Engineering Teams
problem_statement: Manual tuning of agentic heuristics and workflows leads to slow iteration cycles and unoptimized performance across diverse edge cases.

name: Continuous Agentic Self-Improvement and Evaluation
problem_statement: Static evaluation benchmarks fail to capture the drift and complexity of long-running autonomous agents, leading to silent failures in production.

name: Continuous Agentic Self-Improvement and Evaluation
target_segment: Founders and AI Engineering teams building agentic products
problem_statement: Agentic systems lack robust, automated loops to evaluate their strategic decisions and improve heuristics over time, leading to silent drift and execution failure.
